summary: Deprecated audit trail for the old scheduled-tasks bundle. The task paths remain documented, but the parent record is no longer the primary research surface.

setting.name: Telemetry and Maintenance Scheduled Tasks Bundle
setting.casual_explanation: This tweak turns off several Windows scheduled tasks related to compatibility, telemetry, cleanup, feedback, and error-reporting areas.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable these tasks when they want fewer background jobs or a more stripped-down Windows setup.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling the tasks can reduce background activity, but this bundle mixes several different Windows features and the side effects are not the same for every task.
setting.professional_notes: The app uses a scheduled-task batch tweak for seven task paths. Microsoft documents Task Scheduler as the control surface, and the task files exist as Windows system tasks, but the bundle mixes multiple feature areas and at least one task in the current list is version-dependent on the host used for review. This record keeps the bundle in review rather than publishing it as a validated preset.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Task Scheduler start page
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents Task Scheduler as the Windows control surface for scheduled tasks.

evidence.title: Local Windows task file observation
evidence.summary: The listed task paths were checked on the current Windows host. MareBackup, Consolidator, UsbCeip, SilentCleanup, DmClient, and QueueReporting were present; Microsoft Compatibility Appraiser was absent on this machine, confirming version-dependent task presence.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider disables the listed task paths through the batch scheduled-task tweak.

evidence.title: Repo notes for the scheduled-tasks bundle
evidence.summary: The repo documents the current scheduled-task bundle and notes that users should read through the list before switching it.

decision.why: The task paths and app mapping are real, but the bundle mixes several feature areas and includes version-dependent tasks, so it should be reviewed task-by-task before being published as a validated preset.
blocking_issues: The bundle spans multiple Windows feature areas with different side effects.
At least one task path is version-dependent on the current review host.
The current record documents the bundle honestly but does not yet validate it as a safe preset.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Microsoft Learn: Task Scheduler start page: Microsoft documents Task Scheduler as the Windows control surface for scheduled tasks.
validation_proof.notes: Backfilled from evidence_id ms-task-scheduler (official-doc); deprecated audit trail.
